description: Distribution market on the US general tariff rate on Chinese imports as of March 31, 2026. The 15-25% bucket prices at 4%, capturing the tail risk of a sudden tariff escalation before the snapshot date.
question: Will the US tariff rate on China be between 15% and 25% on March 31, 2026?

resolution_logic.summary: Resolves Yes if the general tariff rate on Chinese imports is between 15% and 25% (inclusive of lower bound, exclusive of upper) at 12:00 PM ET on March 31, 2026.

triggers.description: General tariff rate on Chinese imports falls in the 15-25% range
triggers.metric: General import tariff rate on China

resolution_logic.ambiguity_notes: Same general-vs-effective distinction as the 5-15% bucket. At 4% implied probability, market sees low chance of escalation to 15%+ before March 31 — but this bucket captures the tail risk of a sudden executive order. Multiple general rates stack (reciprocal + fentanyl + any new country-specific duties).

catalyst_dates.event: Any new executive orders or trade talk breakdowns before snapshot

cross_platform_link.structural_notes: Kalshi's July 1 tariff distribution has a 10-20% bucket and a 20-30% bucket — overlapping but different boundaries and a 3-month later snapshot date.

related_markets.notes: China tariff 5-15% on March 31 at 92% — dominant bucket. Together: 96% probability rate is between 5-25%.
